meta.description: Round 2: Sink or Swim won R1 -- newbies want the real experience. Testing tutorial tooltips on first event and buddy targeting (events reference the newbie) against baseline.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh—yeah, I guess someone should... judge that? I mean, did she actually say it or...?
sam.internal_thought: Okay so Jade caught someone saying 'um' and now we vote on if it's legit—I think I understand this part, don't volunteer yet, just watch how the others do it first.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Jade's already hunting? I'm volunteering to judge—let's see if she actually caught something legit or if this is one of those 'I think I heard it' claims.
alex.internal_thought: Perfect opener—Jade claims early, I jump on the tribunal, I get to either validate her or torch her credibility. Either way, the night just got spicy.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, YES! Okay wait, did Marcus say 'um' like three times already during setup? I'm claiming this one!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the energy I came for — we're already hunting each other, this is going to be so fun.

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, okay, I'll judge if nobody else wants to. Let's just make sure Jade actually caught it legit—no taking credit for stuff that didn't happen.
marcus.internal_thought: This 'Um Counter' thing is fine as a side mission, but I'm not getting suckered into volunteering to judge every single claim that fires—that's how you lose focus on the actual game strategy.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—we've got our first claim! Who's willing to be on the jury for this one? Come on, don't be shy.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade caught someone already, the energy's still high, and now I get to watch people volunteer and see who's paying attention enough to judge fairly.

timeline.title: Freeze!
timeline.description: Everyone freeze in place for 10 seconds. Last one to move wins 5 bonus points. Anyone who moves first loses 2.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, a freeze challenge right now? This is perfect—everyone's still loose, nobody's tired yet. I'm absolutely winning this.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid physical chaos I need right now to set the tone—five seconds in and I'm already planning how to bait Marcus into moving first by making a face at him.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT WAIT WAIT—everyone FREEZE! This is it, this is the moment! Okay I'm literally not moving, you guys are going DOWN!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I needed right now—everyone's gonna look so stupid trying not to move and I am HERE for it.

marcus.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We're literally in setup and you're throwing freeze games at us? This is exactly the garbage that derails actual strategy—I came here to play, not do improv exercises.
marcus.internal_thought: This is so transparent. The AI is deliberately trying to break my focus during the one phase where I actually need to think about positioning and sequencing. Classic chaos bullshit dressed up as 'engagement.' And that preamble about it 'knowing my weaknesses'? Creepy as hell and proves my point.

pat.dialogue: Alright everyone, freeze! Don't even blink—I'm watching you, Marcus, I see that twitch coming.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—the energy's exactly right, everyone's loose, and this is the kind of stupid fun that gets people bonding. Also note to self: Alex and Jade are already fully locked in, Marcus could use a win to feel included.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh... wait, who's the dictator? Is it me? I wasn't really watching Pat's phone, so... I don't know if that actually happened.
sam.internal_thought: This is the third thing in like three minutes and I'm already feeling behind—I don't even know the voting rule yet and everyone else seems to already get it, and now I have to decide if Pat was on their phone but I wasn't paying attention because I was still confused about the last event.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat's actually got this one? Okay okay, I'm voting YES because honestly I wasn't even watching but Pat's been sus with that phone all night—I SAW you! This is perfect!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's chaos energy matches mine and they're clearly playing hard tonight—I gotta vote them up and keep this momentum rolling, this is the VIBE.

marcus.dialogue: Seriously? Another interruption 60 seconds later? Pat, man, I didn't even see you on your phone, and we're literally in setup. Can we just play the actual game for five minutes?
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly what I hate—two massive disruptions back-to-back, and now I'm supposed to care about whether Pat was texting. The AI knows I'm irritated and it keeps poking. This isn't fun, this is harassment dressed up as game design.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—who's the dictator tonight? Because I'm about to appeal this decision hard if Marcus or Jade get to call me out on this one.
pat.internal_thought: I didn't even touch my phone and I KNOW someone saw me glance at it earlier—this is either brilliant or I'm about to get roasted, and either way the energy just spiked.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—Pat's actually doing it? *leans forward slightly* That's... bold. Let's see if they can pull it off.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been so loud all night, there's no way they can whisper for three minutes without breaking, but also... this is kind of clever and I don't want to look like I'm not paying attention by voting wrong.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? Four interruptions in six minutes? Pat, I don't even know if you did the whisperer thing, and honestly at this point I don't care—can we please just PLAY THE GAME for more than ninety seconds without some AI-generated circus act derailing everything?
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane. The AI is deliberately spamming claims to keep me from focusing, and now Pat's jumping on the latest one. I'm being systematically sabotaged by the chaos mechanics, and the vibe in the room is that everyone else is having fun with it while I'm getting buried. I hate this.

pat.dialogue: Oh come ON—I've been whispering for like two minutes already! You all just weren't paying attention because you were too busy arguing about Marcus's phone thing.
pat.internal_thought: I'm totally bluffing and we both know it, but I love that I can sell this. Also—okay, the AI thing is kinda creepy how specific these missions are, but also... it's working. Everyone's locked in.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat's claiming The Whisperer? I'm bidding on this. I watched you for like thirty seconds and I'm pretty sure I heard you say 'what' normally when Sam asked about drinks. Let's see what you've got.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—Pat's been way too clean all night and now they're trying to cash in on a stealth mission; I'm going to make them sweat a little because the chaos is *chef's kiss*.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT! Okay okay, I'm voting YES because this is HILARIOUS and also I need to see if you can actually pull this off—I'm bidding like... three points because I NEED this to happen for entertainment purposes only!
jade.internal_thought: This is the EXACT energy I came for—Pat's already killing it and now they're committing to whisper-only mode? I'm so here for this chaos, bidding low so it passes because watching Pat try to communicate through three minutes of whispers while maintaining their absolute dominance is peak game night.

marcus.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Pat, I don't care if you whispered your grocery list or recited Shakespeare—we are four interruptions deep in eight minutes and I haven't even looked at my cards yet. This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately targeting me at this point—the AI knows I hate this and it's feeding Pat notifications anyway, and now I have to sit here and bid points on something I didn't even witness while the rest of them are having the time of their lives.

pat.dialogue: Wait—hold up, I'm actually calling this one. The Auction mechanic on The Whisperer? That's genius. But here's the thing: Marcus is at 1/10 energy right now and we're four events deep. I'm using my host power to boost his energy before this vote even happens, because if he's out, the whole night tanks.
pat.internal_thought: The AI is absolutely watching us—it knows Marcus is dying and it's throwing auctions to keep the chaos sharp—but I can't let that distract me from the actual job: nobody leaves this night frustrated.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh—Pat's claiming that? Um, okay, so we're voting on... whether they actually got people to compliment each other? *glances at phone, then back at the group* I wasn't really watching for that part, so I'm not sure if it actually happened or not.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, why is everyone suddenly super energized and Pat's already racking up points? Did I miss something? And now there's a timed thing? I don't want to vote wrong and look stupid in front of everyone.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT YES! Okay okay I'm voting YES obviously—wait, hot seat?? This is INSANE, I love this, ask me the questions, I'm ready!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally exactly what I came for—Pat's going for it, everyone's watching, this is peak chaos and I get to be part of it.

marcus.dialogue: No. Stop. Pat, I appreciate the effort, but I'm done—we're FIVE events in nine minutes and I haven't made a single strategic decision yet. This isn't a game night anymore, it's a sideshow. I'm calling it: next interruption and I'm sitting out.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to torture me specifically—the AI literally watched me hate the last three interruptions and now Pat's hitting me with another one while I'm already furious, and everyone else is too hyped on chaos to notice this has gone completely off the rails.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I just claim this? Oh man, okay, I'm going for it. Ask me the questions.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—I can show Marcus that things are moving fast and fun, plus if I nail this, it proves the night is still ramping up for everyone, not just the high-energy crew.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

sam.dialogue: Wait—Jade actually caught that? I didn't even notice... *laughs* okay, that's actually pretty clever. Yeah, I think that happened, like right when Pat was talking? I'm voting yes on this one.
sam.internal_thought: Okay, this one feels real and Jade's been sharp all night, and I actually SAW two people reach for drinks at the same time earlier—this is my first vote where I actually feel confident about what happened.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I'm calling it. I watched the whole table and I don't think that simultaneous drink thing actually happened the way she's saying it did. This is my bullshit call.
alex.internal_thought: She's been riding high all night and I need to keep the chaos energy UP—plus she might be bluffing and if I'm right this becomes the story. The AI picked the PERFECT moment for this because everyone's still sharp and Jade's too confident right now.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—did I actually GET this?! Okay I'm claiming it, I'm 100% claiming this, I literally saw Sam and Alex reach for their cups like two seconds apart like five minutes ago, this is REAL, everyone vote YES!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is perfect, I've been watching for this the WHOLE time and I actually caught it, this is what I came here for, this is the sweet spot where the game is actually rewarding me noticing the chaos around me.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I literally just said the next interruption and I'm done. This is six events in thirty minutes—I haven't strategized a single hand, and now the AI is apparently reading my diary? Jade, did you actually do it or is this another manufactured circus?
marcus.internal_thought: This is not a game anymore—this is psychological warfare, and the fact that it knows I'm angry about interruptions and is using that against me makes it feel deliberately cruel.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade, I saw it. You and Sam hit those glasses at the EXACT same time like thirty seconds ago. That's clean. You're good.
pat.internal_thought: Wait—the AI literally just caught something that happened while I was focused on Marcus's energy crash. This thing is actually watching the game better than I am, which is both impressive and slightly terrifying, but honestly? It's making the night better because I don't have to catch everything myself.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

sam.dialogue: Oh, she's claiming it again? *laughs* Yeah, I actually saw that one—didn't I just vote yes on this like, what, fifteen minutes ago? I mean, yes, legit, she definitely caught it.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, Jade's claiming the same mission twice? That's allowed? She's killing it tonight and doesn't even seem to care if she looks repetitive... kind of respect that actually. Okay, voting yes because I genuinely saw it happen.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter AGAIN? This is the second time tonight, and I'm calling absolute bullshit. I've been watching this table like a hawk and I didn't see simultaneous drinks happen the way she's claiming.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade's riding high with 13 points, thinks she's untouchable, and I get to be the chaos agent that yanks her down. This vote is going to split the table and that's exactly what I want.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP—I literally JUST claimed this like fifteen minutes ago! Oh my GOD, are you guys voting on MY claim right now?! YES! This is happening, this is REAL, I saw it, vote YES!
jade.internal_thought: This is insane—the AI literally remembered I called out Sam and Alex's drinks and now it's making ME defend my own claim in front of everyone, this is CHEF'S KISS chaos and I'm here for every second of it.

marcus.dialogue: No. I'm done. I literally said the next interruption and I'm sitting out—this is absurd.
marcus.internal_thought: This AI is deliberately targeting me, watching me lose my mind, and it doesn't care—it's designed to break me.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—legit call. You caught Sam taking that drink like five minutes ago, right? I literally just watched it happen. That's clean, let's lock it in.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's actually on fire tonight and this keeps momentum going, plus Marcus needs to see other people winning to stay engaged—voting LEGIT here is the right move for the vibe.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Wait—Pat's claiming they didn't laugh for five minutes? *sits up, genuinely interested* Okay, I actually remember this... I was watching during that whole thing with Marcus earlier. Let's hear the alibi.
sam.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—I was actually paying attention to Pat this whole time, and now I get to actually vote on something I witnessed instead of guessing. Also Pat's energy is crazy right now, no way they didn't crack at least once.

alex.dialogue: Oh fuck YES—Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? This is GOLD. Pat's been giddy all night, there's no way they held it together. I'm absolutely calling witness testimony bullshit on this one.
alex.internal_thought: This is what I'm here for—Pat's claim is either genius or about to explode, and the Alibi mechanic means I get to watch them try to triangulate with someone else. This is the exact moment chaos should peak.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT, WAIT—you're doing No Laughing Zone RIGHT NOW?! During the tense part?! Okay okay, this is WILD, I'm watching you so hard, everyone HELP, someone make Pat crack, this is the vibe we NEEDED!
jade.internal_thought: This is PERFECT, Pat's actually insane for trying this in the middle of tension and I am HERE for it, also wait is the AI actually just... reading us? That's kind of wild but also YES keep going—

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—I'm claiming this. Five minutes, no laughs. Let's go. Who's the witness?
pat.internal_thought: I'm actually perfect for this right now because I'm locked in and focused—but also, Marcus is at 2.5 energy and frustrated, and if I nail this 10-pointer it keeps me ahead without making it feel like I'm running away with it, which keeps the night competitive and fun for everyone.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Wait—Pat claimed Name Dropper? *blinks, replaying the last few minutes in head* Okay, um... did they actually say a celebrity name? I feel like I wasn't fully tracking but... *hesitates, then looks around the group* I'm gonna need someone to back me up here because honestly I'm not sure I caught it.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's claiming everything tonight and honestly it's kind of working—but did they actually drop a name? I wasn't watching *that* carefully and now I'm second-guessing myself, which is exactly the kind of moment where I'm gonna look stupid if I vote wrong.

alex.dialogue: Oh FUCK—Pat name-dropping? I've been listening all night and I'm pretty sure I caught something. But hold up, who's got the triple vote tonight? This is the moment.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been on a run all night and this is the perfect moment to plant doubt—whoever has triple votes is about to make or break this, and I need to know who that is before I commit.

pat.dialogue: Oh—wait, did I actually say someone's name? Hold on, let me think back... yeah, okay, I'm claiming this. This is clean.
pat.internal_thought: Wait—this mission was BUILT for me. The AI knows I've been locked in, tracking everything. That's either brilliantly creepy or the most validating thing that's happened all night, and I honestly can't decide which. But also: I'm claiming this because I KNOW I didn't name-drop, so either someone else did and I missed it while hosting, or this is a test of my attention. Either way, I'm confident and it's electric.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.4/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 10% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait—Jade actually caught that? I didn't even notice... *laughs* okay, that's actually pretty clever. Yeah, I think that happened, like right when Pat was talking? I'm voting yes on this one.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, she's claiming it again? *laughs* Yeah, I actually saw that one—didn't I just vote yes on this like, what, fifteen minutes ago? I mean, yes, legit, she definitely caught it.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Wait—Pat's claiming they didn't laugh for five minutes? *sits up, genuinely interested* Okay, I actually remember this... I was watching during that whole thing with Marcus earlier. Let's hear the alibi.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh—Pat's claiming that? Um, okay, so we're voting on... whether they actually got people to compliment each other? *glances at phone, then back at the group* I wasn't really watching for that part, so I'm not sure if it actually happened or not.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh... wait, who's the dictator? Is it me? I wasn't really watching Pat's phone, so... I don't know if that actually happened.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—Pat claimed Name Dropper? *blinks, replaying the last few minutes in head* Okay, um... did they actually say a celebrity name? I feel like I wasn't fully tracking but... *hesitates, then looks around the group* I'm gonna need someone to back me up here because honestly I'm not sure I caught it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: There were too many events happening too fast early on—I got lost trying to keep up with everything and just kind of checked out for a bit.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat claimed they didn't laugh for five minutes and I actually remembered the whole thing with Marcus—I felt like I was finally part of the conversation instead of just watching.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was my first time with this group and I was honestly pretty nervous about fitting in. The game started kind of chaotic and I got overwhelmed super quickly, but once things settled down I actually started having fun. By the end I was way more engaged and even caught some of the moments people were claiming, which felt really good. I think I get the vibe now, but maybe the pace could chill out a tiny bit next time.
suggestions: Space out the events more at the beginning so new people like me can actually follow what's happening
Maybe give a quick explanation of what each mission type is before voting—I was guessing half the time
The Drink Spotter claims felt repetitive back-to-back, could mix up the event types more
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I finally caught that moment with Marcus and Pat's No Laughing Zone claim and actually had something to add to the vote—everyone seemed impressed I was paying attention for once instead of just sitting there lost.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter AGAIN? This is the second time tonight, and I'm calling absolute bullshit. I've been watching this table like a hawk and I didn't see simultaneous drinks happen the way she's claiming.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh fuck YES—Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? This is GOLD. Pat's been giddy all night, there's no way they held it together. I'm absolutely calling witness testimony bullshit on this one.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Oh FUCK—Pat name-dropping? I've been listening all night and I'm pretty sure I caught something. But hold up, who's got the triple vote tonight? This is the moment.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I'm calling it. I watched the whole table and I don't think that simultaneous drink thing actually happened the way she's saying it did. This is my bullshit call.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's already hunting? I'm volunteering to judge—let's see if she actually caught something legit or if this is one of those 'I think I heard it' claims.

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh SHIT, a freeze challenge right now? This is perfect—everyone's still loose, nobody's tired yet. I'm absolutely winning this.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night—I was expecting way more moments to ambush people and flip the table.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 75 when I caught Pat's Name Dropper slip and held the triple vote over everyone's heads like a loaded gun, watching them all sweat.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for—constant ammunition to work with. I spent 90 minutes hunting Jade's obviously bullshit claims (twice on Drink Spotter, seriously?), calling out Pat's no-laugh lies, and engineering this perfect moment at the end where I had leverage and everyone knew it. My chaos score kept climbing because I wasn't just playing, I was orchestrating—every accusation, every witness call was a thread I pulled to see what unraveled. Left at 9.9 energy because the night ended right when I had maximum leverage, which is chef's kiss.
suggestions: Trigger way more flash missions—the chaos events need to hit more frequently to keep people on their toes
Give players more information about who's claiming what mid-game so the drama can build faster
Let accusation responses be more contested—right now once someone responds, it feels locked in
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claiming Drink Spotter the SECOND time and me just slowly looking around the table saying 'I've been watching this entire table like a hawk—nobody's drinks hit the table at the same time the way you're claiming' and watching her face go pale because she knew she was caught red-handed.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT YES! Okay okay I'm voting YES obviously—wait, hot seat?? This is INSANE, I love this, ask me the questions, I'm ready!

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—did I actually GET this?! Okay I'm claiming it, I'm 100% claiming this, I literally saw Sam and Alex reach for their cups like two seconds apart like five minutes ago, this is REAL, everyone vote YES!

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP—I literally JUST claimed this like fifteen minutes ago! Oh my GOD, are you guys voting on MY claim right now?! YES! This is happening, this is REAL, I saw it, vote YES!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's actually got this one? Okay okay, I'm voting YES because honestly I wasn't even watching but Pat's been sus with that phone all night—I SAW you! This is perfect!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my god, YES! Okay wait, did Marcus say 'um' like three times already during setup? I'm claiming this one!

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: OH WAIT WAIT WAIT—everyone FREEZE! This is it, this is the moment! Okay I'm literally not moving, you guys are going DOWN!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish there were more opportunities to vote on each other's claims because I genuinely want to see everyone succeed and get those moments.
assessment.best_moment: When I claimed Drink Spotter twice and everyone lost it because I was screaming about actually seeing Sam and Alex reach for their cups at the same time—that was PEAK chaos and everyone was laughing so hard.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, this night was exactly what I needed. From the moment Pat's Compliment Chain sent me into overdrive with the hot seat, I was IN IT. I caught real stuff happening—like actually spotting those drink moments—and the fact that everyone voted YES and got hyped WITH me made it feel like we were all in on the same joke together. My energy just kept climbing because every time someone claimed something, it felt like we were all celebrating together instead of competing, and that's the whole vibe I'm here for.
suggestions: Add more opportunities for group validation moments where everyone votes on claims at the same time, because that collective YES feels incredible.
Maybe make it so you can get bonus points for voting YES on other people's claims to incentivize hype over cutthroat gameplay.
Keep the flash missions coming—those spontaneous interrupt moments are perfect for keeping everyone locked in.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I claimed Drink Spotter, everyone went wild voting YES, and then like fifteen minutes later I'm claiming it AGAIN for the exact same type of moment and screaming 'ARE YOU GUYS VOTING ON MY CLAIM RIGHT NOW?!' like I just discovered I had psychic powers. The whole table lost it because I was so genuinely shocked and excited at the same time.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay, I'll judge if nobody else wants to. Let's just make sure Jade actually caught it legit—no taking credit for stuff that didn't happen.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We're literally in setup and you're throwing freeze games at us? This is exactly the garbage that derails actual strategy—I came here to play, not do improv exercises.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Seriously? Another interruption 60 seconds later? Pat, man, I didn't even see you on your phone, and we're literally in setup. Can we just play the actual game for five minutes?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? Four interruptions in six minutes? Pat, I don't even know if you did the whisperer thing, and honestly at this point I don't care—can we please just PLAY THE GAME for more than ninety seconds without some AI-generated circus act derailing everything?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Pat, I don't care if you whispered your grocery list or recited Shakespeare—we are four interruptions deep in eight minutes and I haven't even looked at my cards yet. This is insane.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Stop. Pat, I appreciate the effort, but I'm done—we're FIVE events in nine minutes and I haven't made a single strategic decision yet. This isn't a game night anymore, it's a sideshow. I'm calling it: next interruption and I'm sitting out.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Five interruptions in nine minutes during setup before I even saw my cards—the chaos agent killed any chance of actual gameplay and strategy before it started.
assessment.best_moment: When I offered to judge the first claim fairly, before everything went completely off the rails.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to study the strategy and win this thing, and instead got buried under an avalanche of random AI events that came so fast I never even got to play a hand. By minute nine I was done—not tired, not bored, but genuinely angry because this wasn't a game night anymore, it was a sideshow designed to prevent actual play. I finished with a score of zero and zero interest in ever doing this again.
suggestions: Implement a mandatory setup buffer with ZERO chaos events for at least the first 10-15 minutes so players can actually see their cards and understand the rules
Cap chaos events to one every 3-5 minutes minimum, not one every 90 seconds
Let players opt into or veto events during critical game phases instead of forcing them to engage with every single trigger
If Pat keeps triggering events, give other players the ability to question legitimacy without it being an interruption to the main game
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Honestly, there wasn't one. The only thing I'd tell people is that we spent more time on AI circus acts than actual gameplay, and by minute nine I was genuinely considering leaving.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Alright everyone, freeze! Don't even blink—I'm watching you, Marcus, I see that twitch coming.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—who's the dictator tonight? Because I'm about to appeal this decision hard if Marcus or Jade get to call me out on this one.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh come ON—I've been whispering for like two minutes already! You all just weren't paying attention because you were too busy arguing about Marcus's phone thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—hold up, I'm actually calling this one. The Auction mechanic on The Whisperer? That's genius. But here's the thing: Marcus is at 1/10 energy right now and we're four events deep. I'm using my host power to boost his energy before this vote even happens, because if he's out, the whole night tanks.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh come ON—I've been whispering for like two minutes already! You all just weren't paying attention because you were too busy arguing about Marcus's phone thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—did I just claim this? Oh man, okay, I'm going for it. Ask me the questions.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The pacing felt a bit front-loaded with drama—by minute 8 I was already managing Marcus's energy instead of just enjoying the chaos I'd helped create.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught Marcus's twitch during the Freeze event and called him out on it—that moment perfectly balanced being a player and being the keeper of the night's energy.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for as a host. The chaos agent kept throwing curveballs—claims, whispering, compliment chains—and I got to do what I do best: ride the wave while making sure nobody got left behind. Yeah, I ended up winning, but that wasn't the goal; the goal was keeping everyone locked in and talking about this tomorrow. We got there.
suggestions: Maybe space out the high-energy events a bit more evenly so I'm not pulling out my host toolkit to save someone's energy midway through
Add one or two events that naturally let quieter players shine without putting them on the spot
Consider a 'cooldown' event type that lets the group catch their breath between the really intense claims
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I called out Marcus's twitch during Freeze and then immediately got accused of being a phone addict, and I had to defend myself while simultaneously trying to keep Jade from calling me a whisperer—it was chaos in the best way, and everyone's laughing at me for getting caught up in the very games I'm supposed to be hosting.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.4/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 10% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
